title: Classic Flash
description: The original 3-card Flash. Each player is dealt three cards face down. The highest-ranking hand wins. No wildcards, no twists—pure skill and bluffing.

title: Muflis (Lowball)
description: The lowest hand wins instead of the highest. All standard hand rankings are completely inverted. A 2-3-5 offsuit becomes the best possible hand while a Trail of Aces becomes the worst.

title: AK47 Jackpot
description: All Aces, Kings, 4s, and 7s are universal wildcards. These cards can represent any suit or rank to form the best possible hand. If you hold multiple jokers, they combine sequentially.

title: Sudden Death Discard
description: Players are dealt 4 cards and must discard one before the showdown. Choose wisely—the discarded card is revealed to all players, giving opponents critical information about your hand.

title: Community Pot
description: Each player receives 2 private cards, and 1 community card is placed face-up in the center, shared by all players. Build your best 3-card hand using your 2 private cards plus the community card.

title: Red & Black
description: If all three of your cards are the same color (all red or all black), your hand receives a bonus ranking boost—equivalent to moving up one tier in the hand hierarchy.

title: King's Court
description: All Kings are wild and can substitute for any card. Additionally, any hand containing a natural King (used as its face value) beats an equivalent hand using a King as a wildcard.

title: Blind Swap
description: After the initial deal, each player must pass one card face-down to their left neighbor and receive one from the right—without looking at either card first. High-risk, high-reward chaos.

title: Trio Tornado
description: If any player achieves a Trail (three of a kind), the entire round resets and all players must double the pot. The trail holder keeps their hand while everyone else is re-dealt.

title: One-Eyed Jack
description: The Jack of Spades and Jack of Hearts (the 'one-eyed' Jacks) are wildcards. All other Jacks remain normal. This creates an interesting asymmetry where only 2 of the 4 Jacks carry power.

title: Lowest Card Wild
description: In each player's hand, their lowest-ranked card automatically becomes wild. This means every player has a guaranteed wildcard, but its value depends on the hand they're dealt.

title: Double Exposure
description: Each player must reveal one of their three cards face-up before betting begins. Players choose which card to expose, creating a complex meta-game of bluffing and deduction.

title: Bust Card
description: Before the deal, one random card rank is declared the 'bust' rank. Any player holding a card of that rank must pay a penalty into the pot or fold immediately. Surviving players continue as normal.

title: Stud Flash
description: Cards are dealt one at a time with a betting round after each card. First card face-down, second face-up, third face-down. Players can see partial information about opponents' hands throughout.

title: Lucky 7s
description: All four 7s in the deck are wild. Simple, classic, and easy to remember. Any 7 can substitute for any other card to form the strongest possible hand.

title: Mirror Match
description: Two players are dealt identical hands from two separate decks. They must bet without knowing they hold the same cards. The winner is determined by who bets more aggressively—pure bluffing warfare.

title: Auction Flash
description: After the initial deal, 3 extra cards are placed face-up. Players can auction for the right to swap one of their cards with a face-up card. Highest bidder swaps first, then the next, and so on.

title: Pair Penalty
description: Any hand containing a pair is automatically ranked below a high-card hand. This inverts the usual advantage of pairs and makes players dread matching cards.

title: Joker's Edge
description: Two jokers are added to the deck. If a player is dealt a joker, it's wild. If a joker appears as a community card, all players benefit from it equally, adding a shared wildcard to every hand.

title: Sudden Reveal
description: Players bet without looking at their cards initially. After a set number of betting rounds, all players simultaneously reveal their hands. No peeking allowed—commitment is absolute.
